---
title: "CCGP"
output:
  github_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
---

```{r}
library("rpaleoclim")
library("here")
library("tidyverse")
library("ggpubr")
library("terra")
library("sf")
library("gridExtra")
source("general_functions.R")
source(here("analysis", "genetic_diversity", "genetic_diversity.R"))
ca <- get_ca()
```

# Get heterozygosity Data
```{r}
# Get a list of all .het files in the current directory
het_files <- list.files(here("ccgp_test", "data"), pattern = "\\.het$")

# Extract the species names from the file names
projects <- gsub("\\..*", "", het_files)

# Construct the file paths for the .coords and .het files
coords_files <- paste0(projects, ".coords.txt")
het_files <- paste0(projects, ".het")

df <-
  map(projects, ~{
    het <- read_table(here("ccgp_test", "data", paste0(.x, ".het")))
    het <- het %>% mutate(Ho = `O(HOM)`/N_SITES) %>% rename(SampleID = INDV)
    coords <- read_table(here("ccgp_test", "data", paste0(.x, ".coords.txt")), col_names = FALSE)
    df <- 
      coords %>% 
      rename(SampleID = X1, x = X2, y = X3) %>% 
      left_join(het, by = "SampleID") %>%
      mutate(project = .x)
    return(df)
  }) %>% bind_rows()


```

# Get paleoclim data
```{r}
paleovars <- c("lig", "lgm", "hs1", "ba", "yds", "eh", "mh", "lh", "cur")
# download data
walk(paleovars, ~ {
  paleoclim(.x, "2_5m", region = ext(ca), cache_path = here("data", "env"))
})

# Create a key with layer information
paleokey <- data.frame(
  id = c("cur", "lh", "mh", "eh", "yds", "ba", "hs1", "lgm", "lig", "mis19", "mpwp", "m2"),
  period = c("Current", "Late Holocene: Meghalayan", "Mid Holocene: Northgrippian", "Early Holocene: Greenlandian", "Pleistocene: Younger Dryas Stadial", "Pleistocene: Bølling-Allerød", "Pleistocene: Heinrich Stadial 1", "Pleistocene: Last Glacial Maximum", "Pleistocene: Last Interglacial", "Pleistocene: MIS19", "Pliocene: Mid-Pliocene warm period", "Pliocene: M2"),
  time = c("1979 – 2013", "4.2-0.3 ka", "8.326-4.2 ka", "11.7-8.326 ka", "12.9-11.7 ka", "14.7-12.9 ka", "17.0-14.7 ka", "ca. 21 ka", "ca. 130 ka", "ca. 787 ka", "3.205 Ma", "ca. 3.3 Ma"),
  source = c("CHELSA", "Fordham et al. 2017", "Fordham et al. 2017", "Fordham et al. 2017", "Fordham et al. 2017", "Fordham et al. 2017", "Fordham et al. 2017", "CHELSA", "Otto-Bliesner et al. 2006", "Brown et al. 2018", "Hill 2015", "Dolan et al. 2015")
)

# Print the dataframe
print(df) 
# Define the file names and paths
file_names <- c("LIG_v1_2_5m.zip", "chelsa_LGM_v1_2B_r2_5m.zip", "HS1_v1_2_5m.zip", "BA_v1_2_5m.zip", "YDS_v1_2_5m.zip", "EH_v1_2_5m.zip", "MH_v1_2_5m.zip", "LH_v1_2_5m.zip", "CHELSA_cur_V1_2B_r2_5m.zip")
file_paths <- file.path(here("data", "env"), file_names)

# Load and process the data using purrr
env_list <- purrr::map(file_paths, ~ {
  data <- load_paleoclim(.x)
  data <- crop(data, ca)
  data <- mask(data, ca)
  data
})
names(env_list) <- paleovars
env_stack <- rast(env_list)
cur <- env_list[["cur"]]

env_stack_vals <- extract(env_stack, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
env_stack_vals <- bind_cols(df, env_stack_vals)

ggplot(env_stack_vals) +
  geom_point(aes(x = lgm_16, y = Ho, col = project))
```

# running linear models
```{r}
# use data list to get vars 
env_df_vals <- map(env_list, ~ data.frame(df, extract(.x, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)))
diff_vars <- names(env_list) %>% setdiff("cur")
diff_vals <- map(diff_vars, ~ {
  diff <- env_list[["cur"]] - env_list[[.x]]
  return(data.frame(df, extract(diff, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)))
})
env_df_vals <- c(env_df_vals, diff_vals)
names(env_df_vals) <- c(paleovars, paste0("dif_", diff_vars))

# check for strong correlations
cor_result <- imap(env_df_vals, \(x, idx){
  possibly_cor <- possibly(cor, NA)
  result <- 
    x %>%
    group_by(project) %>%
    summarise(across(starts_with("bio"), ~possibly_cor(Ho, ., use = "complete.obs"))) %>% 
    pivot_longer(contains("bio"), names_to = "var", values_to = "r") %>%
    mutate(timeperiod = !!idx)
}) %>% 
bind_rows() %>%
mutate(timeperiod = factor(timeperiod, levels = names(env_df_vals)))

cor_result %>% filter(abs(r) > 0.6) %>% group_by(var, timeperiod) %>% count() %>% arrange(desc(n))
cor_result %>% filter(abs(r) > 0.6) %>% group_by(var) %>% count() %>% arrange(desc(n)) %>% head()

bio7 <- cor_result %>% filter(var == "bio_7")
ggplot(bio7, aes(x = timeperiod, y = project, fill = r)) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(col = "r") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(bio7, aes(x = timeperiod, y = project, fill = abs(r))) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "rocket", direction = -1) +
  labs(fill = "|r|") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

keep_strongest_correlation <- function(x, threshold) {
  # Calculate the correlation matrix
  cor_matrix <- cor(x, use = "complete.obs")
  
  # Identify sets of correlated variables
  correlated_sets <- caret::findCorrelation(cor_matrix, cutoff = threshold, names = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)

  # For each set of correlated variables, keep only the variable with the strongest correlation with Ho
  x <- map(correlated_sets, function(set) {
    correlations_with_Ho <- sapply(set, function(var) cor(x$Ho, x[[var]], use = "complete.obs"))
    strongest_correlation <- which.max(abs(correlations_with_Ho))
    set <- set[-strongest_correlation]
    x <- x[, !names(x) %in% set]
    return(x)
  }) %>% reduce(intersect)

  return(x)
}

run_lm <- function(project, timeperiod, env_df_vals){
  # subset df
  env_df <- env_df_vals[[timeperiod]] %>% filter(project == !!project)

  # test for collinearity and drop correlated vars as suggested
  bio <- env_df %>% select(contains("bio")) %>% drop_na()
  cormat <- cor(bio)
  safe_cor <- possibly(caret::findCorrelation)
  drop_vars <- safe_cor(cormat, cutoff = 0.6, names = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)
  if (is.null(drop_vars)) return(NULL)
  uncorrelated <- names(bio) %>% setdiff(drop_vars)

  if (length(drop_vars) == 0) {
    uncorrelated <- names(bio)
    correlated <- data.frame(corvars = NA, var = uncorrelated, timeperiod = timeperiod, project = project)
  } else {
    cormat <- cor(bio)
    correlated <- 
      map(uncorrelated, ~ data.frame(corvars = names(which(abs(cormat[.x,]) > 0.5)), var = .x)) %>% 
      bind_rows() %>%
      mutate(timeperiod = timeperiod, project = project)
  }

  if (all(is.na(select(env_df, contains("bio"))))) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  fHo <- formula(paste0("Ho ~", paste0(uncorrelated, collapse = " + ")))
  mod <- summary(lm(fHo, data=env_df))

  result <- 
    data.frame(mod$coefficients) %>% 
    mutate(adj_rsqr = mod$adj.r.squared) %>%
    mutate(var = rownames(.)) %>% 
    filter(var != "(Intercept)") %>%
    left_join(correlated, by = "var") 
  return(result)
}

combos <- expand.grid(project = unique(df$project), timeperiod = names(env_df_vals))
lm_result <- pmap(combos, ~run_lm(project = .x, timeperiod = .y, env_df_vals = env_df_vals), .progress = TRUE) 
lm_df <- 
  lm_result %>%# come back to figure out why some have 0 rows
  compact() %>%
  map(~ {
    if (nrow(.x) == 0)
      return(NULL)
    else
      return(.x)
  }) %>%
  compact() %>% 
  bind_rows()

lm_df <- left_join(lm_df, cor_result)
sigvars <- 
  lm_df %>% 
  select(-corvars) %>% 
  distinct() %>% 
  filter(`Pr...t..` < 0.05) %>% 
  arrange(desc(adj_rsqr)) %>%
  filter(adj_rsqr > 0.3 & r > 0.6) 

sigvars %>% group_by(timeperiod, var) %>% count() %>% arrange(desc(n)) %>% head()
sigvars %>% group_by(var) %>% count() %>% arrange(desc(n)) %>% head()

```

```{r}
plts <-
map(unique(sigvars$timeperiod), ~ {
  sig_df <-
   lm_df %>% 
   filter(timeperiod == .x) %>%
   filter(`Pr...t..` < 0.05, r > 0.6, adj_rsqr > 0.3)

  sig_env_df <- 
    env_df_vals[[.x]] %>% 
    pivot_longer(contains("bio"), names_to = "var") %>%
    filter(project %in% sig_df$project)

  title <- paste0(.x, " (adj R^2 = ", round(mean(sig_df$adj_rsqr, na.rm = TRUE), 2), ")" )

  if (nrow(sig_env_df) == 0) return(NULL)
  plt <-
  ggplot(sig_env_df, aes(value, Ho)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, aes(col = project)) +
    facet_wrap(~var, scales = "free") +
    stat_regline_equation(
      aes(label =  paste(after_stat(eq.label), after_stat(adj.rr.label), sep = "~~~~")),
      formula = y ~ x
    ) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
    stat_cor(label.y = 0) +
    theme_classic() +
    ggtitle(title)

  plt
})

pdf("env_sigvars_with_corvars.pdf", width = 20, height = 15)
walk(plts, print)
dev.off()


plts <-
map(unique(lm_result$model), ~ {
  sig_df <- lm_result %>% filter(model == .x) %>% filter(`Pr...t..` < 0.05)
  sig_env_df <- env_df_vals[[.x]] %>% pivot_longer(contains("bio"), names_to = "var") %>% filter(var %in% unique(sig_df$var))
  title <- paste0(.x, " (adj R^2 = ", round(unique(sig_df$adj_rsqr), 2), ")" )

  if (nrow(sig_env_df) == 0) return(NULL)
  plt <-
  ggplot(sig_env_df, aes(value, Ho)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
    facet_wrap(~var, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +
    stat_regline_equation(
      aes(label =  paste(after_stat(eq.label), after_stat(adj.rr.label), sep = "~~~~")),
      formula = y ~ x
    ) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
    stat_cor(label.y = 0) +
    theme_classic() +
    ggtitle(title)

  plt
})

```
